findings.title: queue/github.ts findLabelAddedAt paginates all issue events with no page count cap
findings.description: findLabelAddedAt used octokit.paginate() with no termination guard, fetching every page of events for an issue. On a long-lived issue with many events this causes unnecessary API quota consumption and latency. Fixed by switching to paginate.iterator() with a MAX_PAGES=10 cap (1000 events), tracking the latest labeled event across all pages.

findings.title: DockerSandboxRunner falls back to in-worktree execution without requiring IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK in non-production environments
findings.description: sandbox.ts only required IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK when NODE_ENV=production. In development/test environments with Docker unavailable, it silently fell back to in-worktree execution. Fixed by removing the NODE_ENV guard — IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK is now required in ALL environments.

findings.title: DockerSandboxRunner forces --user root in Docker container, defeating image-level user hardening
findings.description: buildDockerArgs() passed '--user', 'root' to every docker run invocation. Fixed by removing the --user root flags and letting the image's own USER directive apply.

findings.title: Concurrent cost-log appends can interleave and corrupt costs.json lines
findings.description: appendCostRecord used a bare fs.appendFile with no serialization. Fixed by adding a module-level per-file promise-chain queue so appends serialize without blocking the event loop.

findings.title: runner.ts logCosts uses task.id as sprintId fallback, corrupting sprint-level cost aggregation
findings.description: When PipelineInput.sprintId is absent (all factory.ts task runs), logCosts fell back to input.task.id as the sprintId. Fixed by using 'task:<id>' prefix so task-level costs aggregate separately from sprint costs.

findings.title: audit-runner.ts markFindingsClosed batch path skips per-fingerprint dedup that appendToClosedJson uses
findings.description: Fixed by adding equivalent Set-based dedup to the batch path matching appendToClosedJson.

findings.title: sprint_goal missing required fields return 500 instead of 4xx (fixed by monorepo refactor)
findings.description: Fixed by Phase 1c monorepo refactor (PR #421). The old path src/queue/control-plane.ts no longer exists; packages/orchestrator-core version correctly throws a 400.

findings.title: TaskStore insert never persists the attempts column it relies on (fixed by monorepo refactor)
findings.description: Fixed by Phase 1c monorepo refactor (PR #421). packages/orchestrator-core/src/queue/store.ts uses DEFAULT 0 in the ALTER TABLE migration.

findings.title: listActiveSprints queries all sprints with no LIMIT, causing unbounded memory growth
findings.description: dashboard/server.ts listActiveSprints() had no LIMIT or date cutoff on its SELECT * FROM sprints query. On a long-lived daemon this returns every sprint ever created on each API poll. Fixed by adding LIMIT 500.

findings.title: cancel() is a silent no-op when factory omits abortController, so /stop and /cancel do not kill the pipeline
findings.description: PipelineBridge.spawn() only warned when bootstrap.abortController is undefined, and cancel() called abortController?.abort() — a no-op when undefined. Fixed by creating a default AbortController when the factory omits one, so cancel() at least fires the signal. Inner runner must also carry the signal via bootstrap.input to abort end-to-end.

findings.title: fetchCcusageBlocks does not guard JSON.parse — non-JSON ccusage output kills the monitor permanently
findings.description: usage-monitor.ts called JSON.parse(stdout) with no try/catch. If ccusage emits a startup warning before the JSON payload, JSON.parse throws and PM2 restart loops. Fixed by wrapping JSON.parse in try/catch with a descriptive error including truncated raw output.

findings.title: BLOCK_CAP and WEEKLY_CAP parsed with parseInt but have no NaN guard, silently disabling alerts
findings.description: If CCUSAGE_BLOCK_CAP or CCUSAGE_WEEKLY_CAP are set with comma-formatted numbers (e.g. '1,000'), parseInt returns NaN which propagates through analyzeBlocks() silently. Fixed by adding Number.isFinite() guards with console.warn fallback to documented defaults.

findings.title: TERMINAL_SPRINT_KINDS in dashboard includes 'aborted', a state kind that does not exist in the state machine
findings.description: 'aborted' never appears in the SprintState machine (valid: queued/planning/running/paused/cancelled/completed/failed). The dead entry could mislead future maintainers and silently hide sprints if 'aborted' is added later with different semantics. Fixed by removing 'aborted' from the set.

findings.title: Budget-pause after final task completion permanently deadlocks sprint in 'paused' state
findings.description: When the last task completes and its cost tips over BUDGET_USD, checkBudget transitions the sprint to 'paused' but never sets rateLimitResetAt. tick() returns early from the paused handler on every subsequent call, never reaching the completion check. Recovery requires a manual /continue. NOTE: After investigation, existing tests show this is intentional behavior — budget pause requires operator action to resume. Closing as by-design.
findings.notes: Tests 'budget: sprint pauses when task cost exceeds limit' and 'budget resume: paused sprint can be resumed' confirm manual resume is intended behavior.

findings.title: pendingCount for the rate-limit check uses the stale pre-dispatch task snapshot instead of freshTasks
findings.description: In SprintManager.tick(), 'tasks' is loaded before the dispatch loop. After each dispatch(), tasks are updated to assigned/running in the store, but 'tasks' still shows them as 'pending'. Line 356 computes pendingCount from this stale array, potentially triggering checkRateLimit erroneously.

findings.title: markCompleted receives empty string instead of undefined/null when no PR was opened
findings.description: wireSprintCompletion calls adapter.markCompleted(task, lastPrUrl ?? '', lastTotalTokens). When no PR was opened, '' is passed as prUrl. Implementations storing this field receive '' rather than null/undefined, potentially writing a blank URL to the database.

findings.title: VerifierController hardcodes repoSlug as 'IFleet' regardless of which client repo is being built
findings.description: daemon.ts passes repoSlug: 'IFleet' to VerifierController. When dispatching tasks against client repos, the verifier logs and invariant checks are tagged 'IFleet', producing incorrect verification results and misleading telemetry.

findings.title: Verifier runner has no timeout — a hanging CI subprocess blocks the sprint indefinitely
findings.description: createVerifyRunner() calls runCiKind/runPlaywright/runScreenshotDiff without any AbortSignal or timeout. If any subprocess hangs, the verifier blocks forever, holding the worker slot permanently.

findings.title: autoApproveProposals re-resolves threshold independently, creating a corruptible audit trail
findings.description: splitAndDispatch() resolves the effective threshold at line 123, then autoApproveProposals() re-resolves it at line 180 for the decidedBy audit string. If the env var changes between calls, candidates filtered at one threshold are recorded with a different threshold in goal_proposals.

findings.title: Fleet PAUSED flag path falls back to process.cwd(), causing daemon/cron mismatch
findings.description: fleetRepoRoot() returns process.env['IFLEET_REPO_ROOT'] ?? process.cwd(). If IFLEET_REPO_ROOT is not set, the daemon writes .omc/PAUSED relative to its launch directory while the smoke-runner cron reads from a different cwd. /pause from Discord silently fails to stop new pickups.

findings.title: onCancel swallows all store.updateState errors, masking disk and DB failures
findings.description: control-plane.ts wraps store.updateState() in a bare catch with comment 'row may not exist yet'. This swallows disk-full, WAL corruption, and constraint violation errors, leaving the store in an inconsistent state with no visible signal.

findings.title: Auto-approve leaves a dangling 'approved' proposal when control plane returns accepted=true but no taskId
findings.description: auto-approve.ts skips setResultingTaskId when ack.accepted is true but ack.taskId is falsy. The proposal row was already written as 'approved' by recordProposalDecision. The M5.2-T2 reverse linker has nothing to find, leaving an approved-but-unlinked proposal row.

findings.title: Dashboard startup logs expose full DB filesystem paths including local username
findings.description: dashboard/server.ts logs 'tasksDb=<absolute-path>' and 'stateDb=<absolute-path>' on startup, including the system username (e.g. /home/user/.omc/ifleet/state.db). These paths are written to PM2 logs and any downstream log aggregation.

findings.title: Redundant JS state.kind filter after SQL WHERE clause in listSprintsByStateKind
findings.description: store.ts filters the parsed state against 'kind' in JavaScript after the SQL already filtered with WHERE json_extract. The JS-level guard is dead code for well-formed rows and implies the SQL WHERE is not trusted.

findings.title: lastPausedAt boolean variable in runTickLoop is named as if it holds a timestamp
findings.description: sprint-bridge.ts declares 'let lastPausedAt = false'. The 'At' suffix implies a timestamp but the variable is a boolean. Should be renamed to 'wasPaused' or 'prevTickWasPaused'.

findings.title: forceExplored telemetry count includes already-dropped candidates, overstating the value
findings.description: scorer.ts computes forceExplored as all candidates whose reason starts with 'force-explore', including ones with dropped=true from dedupe. This can make force_explored larger than candidates_kept.

findings.title: resolveTaskIdFromMessage permanently returns null, silently disabling message-to-task routing
findings.description: daemon.ts passes 'resolveTaskIdFromMessage: async () => null' to createDiscordClient with no comment on whether this is deliberate or a placeholder.

findings.title: briefPreview in listTaskQueue always shows JSON envelope bytes, never the human-readable task description
findings.description: dashboard/server.ts slices r.brief to 160 chars. For pipeline tasks, brief is a JSON BridgeBrief envelope, so the dashboard queue view shows raw JSON fragments instead of the task description.

notes: Two audit passes: initial pass (8 fixes from prior open findings) + comprehensive deep scan (20 new findings, 5 fixed immediately + 1 closed as by-design). Total fixes this run: 13. Budget-pause 'deadlock' (3531c250) investigated and confirmed as by-design — tests encode this intentional behavior. sprint.ts change reverted.
